bots.description: Event-driven automations stitched together from triggers and actions.

comingSoon.title: Automations are coming soon
comingSoon.description: Build no-code automations that react to identity events — sign-ins, role changes, risk signals — and run actions in response. This surface isn't available yet; follow the roadmap for availability.

infrastructure.description: Platform health across services, regions, and datastores.

comingSoon.title: Infrastructure health is coming soon
comingSoon.description: Live service, region, and datastore health will surface here once platform telemetry is wired in. For now, use /readyz and /metrics (Prometheus/OTel) from your own observability stack.

authHooks.description: Run a synchronous policy endpoint during sign-in. After credentials verify, Qeet POSTs a signed event to your hook (X-Qeet-Signature, HMAC-SHA256); the hook returns {decision:'allow'|'deny'}. Hooks are bounded by a 3s timeout.

add.title: Add a login hook
add.description: Fired after a password is verified (the “post_login” trigger).
add.failOpenDescription: If the hook errors or times out, allow the sign-in (recommended). Turn off to block sign-ins when the hook is unreachable.

list.title: Hooks
list.emptyDescription: Add a hook above to gate sign-ins with your own policy.

credentials.description: Issue W3C Verifiable Credentials as ES256-signed JWT-VCs (verifiable via the same JWKS) and revoke them. Relying parties verify a presented credential at POST /v1/credentials/verify.

issue.title: Issue a credential
issue.description: The subject is the credentialSubject id (user uuid, DID, or email). Claims is a JSON object; TTL 0 = non-expiring.
issue.subject: Subject
issue.signedLabel: Signed credential (JWT-VC) — give this to the subject:

verify.title: Verify a credential
verify.description: Paste a JWT-VC to check its signature, expiry, and revocation.

list.title: Issued credentials
list.description: Registry of credentials issued by this tenant.

agents.description: First-class identities for AI agents / MCP clients, and the primitives that keep them accountable to a human: sponsorship, Shadow-AI discovery, ephemeral tokens, and a tenant-wide kill-switch.

create.title: Create an agent
create.description: The secret is shown once. Scopes are space-separated; token lifetime is clamped to 60–3600s. You’re recorded as the agent’s sponsor — its accountable human owner — and can transfer that later if it changes hands.
create.name: Name
create.secretNotice: Agent {{name}} created — copy its credentials now (the secret won’t be shown again):

list.title: Agents
list.emptyDescription: Create an agent above to issue it ephemeral, scoped tokens.

confirm.suspendAllDescription: Their tokens will stop working immediately.

sponsor.title: Sponsor transfer
sponsor.description: Every agent has a named human sponsor. When a sponsor is offboarded, reassign everything they own to a new sponsor in one call — no agent is left ownerless.
sponsor.count_one: {{count}} agent sponsored by this user will move.
sponsor.count_other: {{count}} agents sponsored by this user will move.

confirm.title: Transfer {{count}} agent(s) to the new sponsor?
confirm.label: Transfer

shadow.title: Shadow AI discovery
shadow.description: OAuth clients that picked up a machine grant type (client_credentials / token-exchange) without ever going through the agents/service-accounts registry — unmanaged automation acting under this tenant, ranked by live grants.
shadow.emptyDescription: Every machine-grant OIDC client has been acknowledged.

webhooks.description: HTTP endpoints we POST events to. Every delivery is HMAC-SHA256 signed via the X-Signature header and retried with exponential backoff.

create.title: New webhook subscription
create.description: We'll POST signed JSON to your URL for each selected event.
create.urlHelp: Must respond 2xx within 10 seconds; we retry up to 10 times with exponential backoff.

webhookDetail.description: Webhook endpoint configuration and delivery history.

info.title: Endpoint details
info.secretHelp: HMAC-SHA256 — verify the X-Signature header with this key.

deliveries.title: Recent deliveries
deliveries.description: Last 50 attempts. Failures are retried with exponential backoff.
deliveries.emptyDescription: When this webhook fires, attempts will appear here.

sample.title: Sample payload
sample.description: POSTed to the URL above with an HMAC-SHA256 signature in the X-Signature header.
